report:
## Your Health Assessment

Thank you for taking the time to complete this assessment. The patterns that emerge from your answers paint a coherent picture, and the fact that you're thinking proactively about your health before pregnancy tells us a lot about the kind of care you bring to things that matter. Let's walk through what stands out.

### Your Energy System Is Running on Fumes

An energy level of 1 out of 10, difficulty waking in the morning, brain fog, and crashing after lunch are not separate problems. They point to the same underlying issue: your body is struggling to produce and sustain cellular energy. When energy metabolism falters, it touches everything, from how clearly you think to how well your gut functions to how deeply you sleep. The afternoon crash is particularly telling. After a meal, your body should be fueled, not depleted. When the opposite happens, it often means the body has been leaning on stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline to keep going, and those hormones temporarily drop once food arrives, leaving you running on empty. This is a central pattern in your assessment, and nearly everything else connects back to it.

### Nighttime Waking to Eat Points to Blood Sugar and Liver Stress

Waking up in the middle of the night specifically to eat is one of the more revealing answers in your assessment. During sleep, the liver is responsible for steadily releasing stored glucose to keep your brain and body fueled. When it can't do this effectively, blood sugar drops, stress hormones spike to compensate, and you wake up, often with a craving for carbohydrates or something salty. This pattern frequently ties back to metabolic stress and sometimes to the burden that gut-derived inflammation places on the liver over time. For someone preparing for pregnancy, nighttime blood sugar stability is especially important, as it reflects how well the body manages energy under resting conditions.

### Cold Extremities Suggest Your Metabolism Needs Support

Frequently feeling cold, especially in the fingertips, toes, and nose, is a classic sign that thyroid-driven metabolism may be underperforming. Thyroid hormones govern how much heat and energy your cells produce. When they're insufficient, the body conserves resources by pulling warmth away from the extremities. This connects directly to your fatigue, your morning difficulty, and your brain fog. Low thyroid function also slows gut motility, reduces digestive enzyme secretion, and impairs nutrient absorption, all of which feed into the other patterns showing up in your answers.

### Your Gut Is Sending Clear Signals

The combination of bowel pain and excessive smell or messiness in your stools tells us your gut is not breaking down or processing food efficiently. Poorly digested food can fuel microbial overgrowth, produce irritating byproducts, and drive inflammation that extends well beyond the digestive tract. Your current diet, while not unusual, includes several factors that may be compounding this. Oatmeal is high in fiber and can be difficult to digest, especially when gut function is already compromised. Sandwich bread and pasta often contain fortified iron and various additives. Chips are typically cooked in seed oils. These are not dramatic dietary missteps, but in the context of an already struggling system, they add up. With pregnancy on the horizon, gut health takes on even greater importance. Nutrient absorption, immune regulation, and hormonal balance all depend on a well-functioning digestive system, and these are the very foundations that support a healthy pregnancy.

### These Patterns Are Connected, Not Coincidental

What we see in your answers is not a random collection of complaints. Low energy, cold extremities, nighttime waking, digestive issues, brain fog, and afternoon crashes are all threads in the same web. Impaired energy metabolism makes the gut more vulnerable. A struggling gut burdens the liver and increases inflammation. That inflammation suppresses thyroid function further. And round it goes. The good news is that because these issues share common roots, addressing the deeper drivers can create improvements across many areas at once. This is especially meaningful when your goal is to prepare your body for pregnancy, because the same foundations that resolve these symptoms are the ones that create a thriving environment for conception and beyond.

---

A quiz like this can reveal important patterns, but it can only go so far. Your situation has layers that deserve a real conversation. If you'd like to explore what's going on in more depth, we offer a free consultation with someone on our team who understands these patterns and can help you think through what meaningful next steps might look like, especially as you prepare for this next chapter. There's no cost and no obligation. You can book using the button below whenever you're ready.
